summary: Phase 8.A.3 (build-pipeline extension — dual-build CI + releases/index.json publish + auto-redirect on page load + Share This Run button + history.replaceState canonicalization) approved. Phase 8.A.3 is the FIRST phase in the project explicitly marked NOT-sandbox-verifiable in its memo (the live dual-build is verified on the deploy.yml CI run after this commit lands; Phase 8.B does the cross-runtime + cross-OS verification). However, the script logic, main.ts wiring, and pure-function helpers ARE sandbox-verifiable and are exercised by 22 net-new unit tests + 1 regression test. Concrete deliverables: (1) scripts/build-dual.mjs — runs `npx vite build` twice with different `--base` values (`/icefall/` and `/icefall/releases/<commit-12>/`); leaves `dist/` populated as the latest tree (so vite preview for e2e still works) AND assembles `dist-final/` with both trees merged. The 12-character commit-short is the forever-collision-resistant pin from memo addendum B4. (2) scripts/publish-dual.mjs — pure mergeReleaseIndex(prior, newEntry) logic + fetch (with advisory-A7 fallback to local dist-final/releases/index.json on 404 / DNS failure / parse failure / bootstrap) + write. Computes rulesetVersion + atlasBinaryHash directly via the existing computeDefinePayload helper from scripts/vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash.mjs (avoids brittle bundle-grep extraction of build-time constants). Re-deploys of the same commit-short replace the existing entry rather than duplicating; new entries are prepended (newest-first per memo addendum B5). (3) scripts/publish-dual.d.mts — TypeScript declaration file for the .mjs script (mirrors the existing pattern from scripts/vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash.d.mts). (4) src/main.ts new applyRouting(): Promise<boolean> function called at top of bootstrap(): URL has no `?run=` → boot fresh; URL has `?run=` matching current build → history.replaceState canonicalization to formatShareUrl output (with FULL existing href passed in to preserve unrecognized query params like `?mode=replay`); URL has `?run=` NOT matching → fetch releases/index.json with 5s AbortController timeout + graceful 404/DNS/parse fallback, then decideRouting; on `redirect` → window.location.replace + return true (skip rendering); else → render diagnostic with __ROUTER_AUTO_DECISION_KIND__ window flag set. (5) src/main.ts new 'Share this run' diagnostic section: two text inputs (seed + mods comma-separated), button label varies by deploy context per advisory A3 (`Mint share URL (pinned to current build)` on /icefall/ vs `Mint share URL (pinned to commit <short>)` on /icefall/releases/<commit>/), on-click computes fingerprintFull → formatShareUrl → sets <pre> output → sets window.__SHARE_URL__ → best-effort navigator.clipboard.writeText. (6) .github/workflows/deploy.yml extended: replaced `npm run build` step with `node scripts/build-dual.mjs --commit-short=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) --dist-final=dist-final`; bundle-size budget step now reads from dist-final/assets/; new publish-dual step runs after the bundle gate with --prior-index-url=https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/releases/index.json; bundle-report upload now includes dist-final/releases/index.json; pages-artifact upload now uses dist-final instead of dist. (7) .gitignore extended: dist-build-*/ and dist-final/ added so dual-build intermediate trees don't get accidentally committed. (8) eslint.config.js extended: dist-build-* and dist-final added to the top-level ignores list so a developer who runs build-dual locally then runs lint doesn't hit ~423 errors on the minified production JS (per code-review-phase-8-A-3.md B1). (9) Window flag declarations added: __SHARE_URL__, __ROUTER_AUTO_DECISION_KIND__, __URL_CANONICALIZED__. (10) New tests: tests/build/publish-dual.test.ts (21 tests covering mergeReleaseIndex happy path + bootstrap + re-deploy + 6 validation rejections; tryParsePriorIndex with malformed JSON / non-object / schema mismatch / partial-write defense / valid prior; parseArgs required-arg + optional --prior-index-url + malformed-arg). New regression test in tests/router/url-parse.test.ts: 'preserves unrecognized query params (e.g. ?mode=replay) when canonicalizing' — pins the B2 fix that applyRouting passes the FULL href to formatShareUrl to preserve `?mode=replay` and other future query params. All sandbox gates green: 1154 tests passing across 81 test files (was 1132/80 in Phase 8.A.2b; +22 tests / +1 file from publish-dual.test.ts + regression test); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build 139.20 KB raw / 41.30 KB gzipped (was 132.38/39.16 in 8.A.2b = +6.82 KB raw / +2.14 KB gzipped from auto-redirect + Share button + history.replaceState wiring). Well under the 110 KB CI gate. Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST). Smoke-tested build-dual locally: produces dist/ with /icefall/ base AND dist-final/releases/<commit>/ with /icefall/releases/<commit>/ base — verified via grep on the emitted index.html files.
suggested_commit_message: phase-8.A.3: build-dual + publish-dual + auto-redirect + Share This Run + history.replaceState (memo decisions 5+7+11+17 + addendum B5+B9 + advisory A3+A7); code-review B1+B2 fixed; Phase 8.B unblocked

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 8.A.3's build-pipeline extension + auto-redirect + Share This Run wiring preserve every Phase 7.B + 8.A.1 + 8.A.2a + 8.A.2b invariant byte-for-byte (cross-runtime golden chain unchanged; existing test goldens preserved; rules-files-reachability gate holds; Phase 8 layer anti-cycle holds — none of router/share/save/verifier reachable from src/sim/harness.ts) and that every gate is green at the time of phase approval. Phase 8.A.3 is explicitly NOT-sandbox-verifiable per the memo (the live dual-build is verified on the deploy.yml CI run after this commit lands; Phase 8.B does cross-runtime + cross-OS verification). The sandbox-verifiable pieces (script pure logic, main.ts wiring, lint/typecheck/test/build) ARE green. Phase 8.A is now complete (8.0 → 8.A.1 → 8.A.2a → 8.A.2b → 8.A.3); Phase 8.B is unblocked.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 1154 passed across 81 test files (was 1132/80 in Phase 8.A.2b). Net change: +22 tests / +1 file. Breakdown: tests/build/publish-dual.test.ts (21 tests); tests/router/url-parse.test.ts (+1 regression test for the B2 fix — '?mode=replay' preservation through canonicalization). The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) is preserved verbatim.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The eslint.config.js extends the top-level ignores list to cover the dual-build intermediate trees (dist-build-*/) and the final merged tree (dist-final/) — preventing the ~423-error blockade a developer would hit by running build-dual locally and then npm run lint (per code-review B1 fix). Verified live with a stale dist-build-deadbeef0000/ tree present.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. The new types from publish-dual.d.mts (ReleaseEntry, ReleaseIndex, mergeReleaseIndex signature) integrate cleanly. The main.ts wiring uses the existing FingerprintInputs + RoutingDecision types unchanged.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.js = 139.20 KB raw / 41.30 KB gzipped. Delta from Phase 8.A.2b (132.38 / 39.16): +6.82 KB raw / +2.14 KB gzipped from the auto-redirect logic + Share This Run button + history.replaceState canonicalization in src/main.ts. Bundle is well under the 110 KB CI gate (~70 KB headroom for Phase 9 polish).
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: User-visible additions in 8.A.3: (1) Auto-redirect on page load — when a URL with `?run=` matches the current build's fingerprint, the URL bar canonicalizes to the formatShareUrl form (preserving extra params like ?mode=replay per the B2 fix); when it doesn't match, the page fetches releases/index.json (5s timeout + graceful fallback) and either redirects to the matching releases/<commit>/ subtree or surfaces an error via the diagnostic page's __ROUTER_AUTO_DECISION_KIND__ window flag. (2) Share This Run diagnostic section — user enters seed + mods, clicks the button (label varies by deploy context per advisory A3), the page mints the canonical URL via formatShareUrl + copies via navigator.clipboard.writeText (best-effort). The bare URL (no `?run=`) on the live deploy renders identically to Phase 8.A.2b. The auto-redirect path is now LIVE on every page load with `?run=` — pre-8.A.3 deploys would have 404'd on releases/index.json; post-8.A.3 the file exists and the redirect works. Cross-runtime e2e cannot run in-sandbox (Playwright browser binaries not preinstalled — established X.B sandbox limitation per docs/PHASES.md and the deploy.yml `npx playwright install --with-deps` step on the ubuntu-24.04 runner); the new __SHARE_URL__ + __ROUTER_AUTO_DECISION_KIND__ + __URL_CANONICALIZED__ window flags will be exercised on the deploy.yml CI run that lands this commit.

build_dual_script_works_locally.evidence: scripts/build-dual.mjs implements the dual-build per memo decision 7 + 17 + addendum B4. Smoke-tested locally with `node scripts/build-dual.mjs --commit-short=deadbeef0000 --dist-final=/tmp/dist-final-test`: produces (a) dist/ with `<script src="/icefall/assets/index-D8PEhQ2Y.js">` (latest tree, base = /icefall/) AND (b) /tmp/dist-final-test/releases/deadbeef0000/index.html with `<script src="/icefall/releases/deadbeef0000/assets/index-Dp8nJZwz.js">` (per-release tree, base = /icefall/releases/deadbeef0000/). The cpSync-based copy preserves directory structure correctly. The dist/ tree is left populated so vite preview for e2e tests in the same CI job continues to work.

publish_dual_pure_logic.evidence: scripts/publish-dual.mjs implements mergeReleaseIndex(prior, newEntry): ReleaseIndex (pure function, prepends new entry, replaces same-commitShort entries, validates per-field regex set) + tryParsePriorIndex(text): ReleaseIndex | null (defensive parser; returns null on malformed JSON / non-object / schema mismatch / partial-write defense — failures are logged so 8.A.3 deploys can diagnose). Computes rulesetVersion + atlasBinaryHash directly via computeDefinePayload from scripts/vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash.mjs (no brittle bundle-grep extraction). Advisory-A7 bootstrap-from-local fallback: when fetch fails or returns non-200, falls back to readLocalIndex(distFinal); when that also returns null, seeds an empty index. Re-deploy of same commit-short replaces existing entry. tests/build/publish-dual.test.ts (21 tests) covers happy path + bootstrap + re-deploy + validation rejections.

auto_redirect_on_page_load.evidence: src/main.ts new applyRouting(): Promise<boolean> function called at top of bootstrap() (lines 1006-1101). Behavior: (a) URL has no `?run=` → returns false, sets __ROUTER_AUTO_DECISION_KIND__ = 'no-run-param', boot fresh. (b) URL has `?run=` matching current build → calls history.replaceState with the canonical formatShareUrl(inputs, fp, logWire, href) — pass FULL href to preserve unrecognized query params like ?mode=replay (B2 fix); sets __URL_CANONICALIZED__ flag. (c) URL has `?run=` NOT matching → fetches releases/index.json from buildReleasesIndexUrl with 5s AbortController timeout; on success calls decideRouting; on 'redirect' → window.location.replace + return true (skip rendering); on 'error' → render normally with __ROUTER_AUTO_DECISION_KIND__ set to the error kind so the diagnostic page can surface it (Phase 9 polish adds a banner UI). The fetch failure path falls through gracefully — pre-8.A.3 deploys without releases/index.json wouldn't have made it here, and post-8.A.3 the file exists.

share_this_run_button.evidence: src/main.ts new 'Share this run' diagnostic section (lines 654-749) per memo decision 11 + advisory A3 + addendum B9. Two text inputs (seed + mods comma-separated). Button label per advisory A3: regex `/\/releases\/[0-9a-f]{12}\/$/.test(baseUrl)` distinguishes the two contexts; latest/ shows 'Mint share URL (pinned to current build)'; releases/<commit>/ shows 'Mint share URL (pinned to commit <short>)'. On click: parses seed + mods (split on comma, trim, filter empty), constructs FingerprintInputs with current build's commitHash + rulesetVersion, calls fingerprintFull → formatShareUrl, sets <pre> output, sets window.__SHARE_URL__, calls navigator.clipboard.writeText (best-effort — silent on failure since the URL is still visible in the <pre>). Invalid seeds (NUL or surrogate) surface via the fingerprintFull throw → caught and shown in the <pre>.

history_replacestate_canonicalization.evidence: applyRouting calls window.history.replaceState(null, '', canonical) when the URL bar's form differs from the canonical formatShareUrl output (lines 1037-1054). Per the B2 fix from code-review-phase-8-A-3.md: the formatShareUrl baseUrl is the FULL existing href (not origin + pathname), so unrecognized query params like ?mode=replay survive the canonicalization. Verified by tests/router/url-parse.test.ts new regression test 'preserves unrecognized query params (e.g. ?mode=replay) when canonicalizing'. The replaceState call is wrapped in try/catch — sandboxed iframes that lock down the History API silently degrade (the URL just won't canonicalize).

deploy_yml_dual_build_extension.evidence: .github/workflows/deploy.yml extended (lines 35-78). Order: lint → typecheck → unit tests → dual-build (`node scripts/build-dual.mjs --commit-short=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) --dist-final=dist-final` — produces dist/ for e2e + dist-final/ for deploy) → bundle-size budget against dist-final/assets/ → publish-dual (`node scripts/publish-dual.mjs --commit-short=... --published-at=$(date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ') --dist-final=dist-final --prior-index-url=https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/releases/index.json`) → bundle-report upload (now includes dist-final/releases/index.json) → playwright install → cross-runtime e2e (uses dist/ via vite preview, unchanged) → configure pages → upload-pages-artifact (now uses dist-final). The dual-build leaves dist/ populated so the e2e step continues to work without modification.

code_review_b1_b2_fixed.evidence: artifacts/code-review-phase-8-A-3.md verdict: NEEDS-REWORK with 2 blocking issues. (B1) eslint.config.js's top-level ignores list didn't cover dist-build-*/ and dist-final/, so a developer running build-dual locally and then `npm run lint` hit ~423 errors on minified production JS. Fixed at eslint.config.js:49 by extending the ignores list. Verified: lint passes with a stale dist-build-deadbeef0000/ directory present. (B2) applyRouting's history.replaceState canonicalization called formatShareUrl(inputs, fp, logWire, origin + pathname) which DROPPED unrecognized query params — most importantly ?mode=replay which the diagnostic page's Replay-this-run section reads later. Fixed at src/main.ts:1043 by passing the FULL href instead of origin + pathname. Regression test added at tests/router/url-parse.test.ts:188-205. The 10 non-blocking nits (N1-N3 plus other suggestions) are documented in the code review and carried forward.

all_sandbox_gates_green.evidence: npm run lint: 0 errors. npm run typecheck: 0 errors. npm run test: 1154 passed across 81 test files (was 1132/80 in Phase 8.A.2b; +22 tests / +1 file). npm run build: 139.20 KB raw / 41.30 KB gzipped (well under the 110 KB CI gate; +6.82 KB raw / +2.14 KB gzipped vs Phase 8.A.2b). E2e cannot run in-sandbox (established X.B limitation); the cross-runtime golden chain (incl. REPLAY_DIGEST entry from 8.A.2a) is exercised in Node via src/core/self-test.test.ts (4 tests) and tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts (7 tests) — preserved unchanged. tests/build/rules-files-reachability.test.ts: 7 tests pass (the 36-file reachable-set count holds; src/router/, src/save/, src/share/, src/verifier/ remain NOT reachable from src/sim/harness.ts — addendum B8 anti-cycle defense intact).

phase_8_a_complete.evidence: Phase 8.A is now complete: 8.0 (planning gate) → 8.A.1 (drift sweep + scaffolding) → 8.A.2a (codec + verifier foundation) → 8.A.2b (router + save + diagnostic UI wiring) → 8.A.3 (build-pipeline extension + auto-redirect + Share This Run + canonicalization). Memo addendum B9 deferrals correctly held across the split: 'Share This Run' button + window.__SHARE_URL__ flag + history.replaceState canonicalization + auto-redirect on page load + releases/index.json publish step are ALL landed in 8.A.3. The next phase is 8.B (live-deploy + cross-runtime + cross-OS verification per the established X.B pattern).

follow_ups_carried_forward: S2 from Phase 7.A.2b (drift-sweep candidate, deferred yet again) — tests/sim/boss-fsm.test.ts:47-48 and tests/sim/turn.test.ts:419-447 construct synthetic Monster fixtures with the OLD boss stats (hpMax: 40, atk: 9, def: 5). Phase 8.A.3 does not touch these test files; future drift sweep is the next opportunity.

Code-review N1-N3 + S1-S5 + O1-O3 from artifacts/code-review-phase-8-A-3.md (advisory-only, all non-blocking) — dead `dirname` import in publish-dual.mjs (the `void dirname;` workaround is fine but cleaning it up would be cleaner); missing computeDefinePayload contract pin (publish-dual depends on the helper's signature; a future refactor of vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash.mjs that changes computeDefinePayload's return shape would break publish-dual silently — a defensive smoke test could pin the call surface); share-button input validation could pre-validate the seed before calling fingerprintFull (currently the throw is caught and shown but a friendlier inline error would help); applyRouting has zero unit tests (DOM-dependent; the JSDOM-friendly extraction of the canonicalization logic to a pure helper would enable testing without main.ts wiring); Phase 9 polish should add a banner UI for the auto-redirect error path so users see what happened instead of having to inspect window flags. None are gating.

Phase 8.B scope (live-deploy + cross-runtime + cross-OS verification per docs/PHASES.md) inherits this 8.A.3 work. The Phase 8.B Playwright tests will assert: (a) auto-redirect from latest/ to releases/<commit>/ works on chromium/firefox/webkit; (b) the Share This Run button mints a working URL on each browser; (c) history.replaceState canonicalization fires when expected (URL bar updates to canonical form); (d) cross-OS atlas equality matrix re-asserts byte-equality on the unchanged atlas binary.
follow_ups_observations: Phase 8.A.3 is the FIRST phase in the project explicitly marked 'NOT sandbox-verifiable' in the memo. The dual-build pipeline must be exercised on the live GH-Pages deploy before its correctness can be asserted end-to-end (the bundle size, the dist-final/ structure, and the publish-dual fetch + write are all sandbox-testable, but the actual landing of releases/<commit>/index.html on the live host is observable only post-deploy). The decision to land 8.A.3 with this caveat — relying on the deploy.yml CI run + Phase 8.B's live-deploy verification — was anticipated by the memo's decision 17 phase split.

The auto-redirect path is now LIVE on every page load with `?run=` in the URL. Pre-8.A.3 deploys would have 404'd on the releases/index.json fetch (the file didn't exist); post-8.A.3 the file exists (bootstrapped on the first 8.A.3 deploy via publish-dual's empty-index fallback). The 5s AbortController timeout caps the worst-case page-load delay if the GH-Pages CDN is slow; the graceful fallback (set __ROUTER_AUTO_DECISION_KIND__ = 'error', render normally) ensures the user always sees the page even if routing fails.

The B2 fix (canonicalization preserves ?mode=replay and other unrecognized params) is the kind of integration bug that's only visible when two parts of the system depend on the same URL bar — applyRouting writes the URL, the Replay-this-run section reads it. Without the regression test, a future refactor that 're-cleans up' the canonicalization to use `origin + pathname` would silently break the replay viewer. The new test pins the param-preservation property at the formatShareUrl layer so the bug can't recur.

Phase 8.B is unblocked. Phase 8.B's deliverables per docs/PHASES.md (the established X.B pattern): (a) live-deploy verification — push triggers .github/workflows/deploy.yml; the dual-build runs; the publish-dual step extends releases/index.json; the upload-pages-artifact step deploys dist-final to GH Pages. (b) cross-runtime Playwright on the live deploy — tests assert auto-redirect works on chromium/firefox/webkit, Share This Run button mints the URL, history.replaceState fires when expected, REPLAY_DIGEST self-test passes in all three browsers. (c) cross-OS atlas equality matrix re-asserts byte-equality on the unchanged atlas binary (the matrix is an inheritance from Phase 4.B; the atlas binary is unchanged in Phase 8 so the matrix continues to be green by inheritance). (d) 'old run after master moves on' deferred-assertion (the full check at the next rulesetVersion-bumping commit; the synthetic stale-fingerprint routing test exercises the path now).
next_phase: Phase 8.B — live-deploy + cross-runtime + cross-OS verification. Per docs/PHASES.md (the established X.B pattern): the next push to master triggers .github/workflows/deploy.yml; the dual-build (8.A.3) runs; the publish-dual step extends releases/index.json with the new entry (advisory-A7 fallback to local on first deploy); the upload-pages-artifact step deploys dist-final to GitHub Pages with both latest/ and releases/<commit>/ paths working. Acceptance criteria: (a) live URL serves the diagnostic page with the new Verify-pasted-log + Save-slots + Replay-this-run + Share-this-run sections from 8.A.2 + 8.A.3; (b) cross-runtime Playwright on chromium/firefox/webkit asserts auto-redirect works for a synthetic stale fingerprint, Share This Run button mints a URL with __SHARE_URL__ set, history.replaceState canonicalizes when fingerprint matches, REPLAY_DIGEST self-test passes; (c) cross-OS atlas equality matrix re-asserts byte-equality on the unchanged atlas binary (8ca99389...); (d) the 'stranger on a different machine' property is exercised (the deploy URL renders the same diagnostic surface to any visitor). Phase 8.B is the smallest-scope X.B in the project to date — most verification is via the existing deploy.yml infrastructure; the only sandbox-resolvable work is the in-sandbox add (or no add, if 8.A.3's coverage is sufficient). The next iteration of the agent loop should pick up phase-8.B and run the verification sprint per QUALITY_GATES.md.
